Stored Procedures on Microsoft SQL Server

Whenever possible or desirable, the connector generates stored procedures for most basic I/O operations. A stored procedure encapsulates one or more SQL statements and is stored in compiled format. During execution, the parsing phase can be skipped.

For more information about stored procedures, see the Microsoft SQL Server documentation.

For each table, a number of procedures are generated.

Table 1. Generated Stored Procedures

Procedure name

Meaning

TableName _FET

Fetch the record specified by the primary key

TableName _DEL

Delete the record specified by the primary key

TableName _INS

Insert a record

TableName _UPD

Update the record specified by the primary key

Creating Stored Procedures

Stored procedures can be created if the procedures connector option is on. They are created either on-the-fly if the table is not yet present in the DBMS, or by the scripts generated by the Create Table facility. If created on the fly, the connector does not function correctly if the procedures option is on and there are no procedures present in the DBMS.

Stored Procedure Parameters

MS-SQL has a maximum of 255 parameters.

Parameters are defined for stored procedures when the stored procedure is created. The following definitions are made:

  • Parameters to use
  • Data types of each parameter
  • SQL statement
  • Position of the parameters in the SQL statement

You must provide a real value for every parameter when a stored procedure is executed.

Parameters for field lists used in select, insert, and update are:

@field1, @field2, ..., @fieldn

Parameters for where clauses in select, delete, and update:

@where1, @where2, ..., @wheren

The parameter that specifies the inclusion of the U_VERSION field in the where clause is @uversion.

Segmented Fields

If the automatic field list includes segmented fields (packing codes SC or SU), stored procedures are not used, even if enabled. Instead SQL is used to retrieve all the data except the segmented field data.

Related Links
  • Segmented Fields: String Packing Codes (SC and SU)

    Segmented fields provide an alternative storage method for large amounts of data that would be impractical to store in a normal database column. Large data objects can include audio- and video clips, or objects that functionally belong to other applications but are under the control of Uniface. Such objects are referred to as Binary Large Objects (BLOBs).

  • Field Lists

    A component's field list determines how Uniface manages the fields when retrieving occurrences into the component at runtime. The field list includes not only the fields that are part of the component data structure, but also other fields that may be required, for example, because they are referenced in ProcScript or part of a key.

  • procedures

    Specify whether the connector will use stored procedures to update, insert, delete, and fetch data, or use SQL instead.
